title: Skip Pages cutover for worker-target apps
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
`deploy.sh` ran the Pages-only cutover (`deploy-app.js --phase=cutover`, which calls `getPagesProject apps-<sub>`) for **every** app. Worker apps have no Pages project, so it 404s:

```
cutover failed: Get project apps-websim failed: Project not found (8000007)
```

Worker apps (e.g. websim) already bind `<sub>.pollinations.ai` themselves via `wrangler.toml` custom-domain routes — same as enter/gen — so the Pages cutover doesn't apply to them.

- Skip `--phase=cutover` when `deployTarget == worker`
- Fixes the red `Deploy App (Automatic)` on any run that deploys a worker app; websim itself was never down (HTTP 200)

Surfaced by the app-deploy that ran on the brand-assets merge; unrelated to that change.

gist.headline: Smooth worker deploys
gist.blurb: Fixed a hiccup in our deployment pipeline where worker apps were getting confused with Pages apps. CI/CD is back to being beautifully green!
gist.summary: Updated the deployment script to skip the Cloudflare Pages cutover phase for worker-target apps like websim, preventing a 404 error during CI/CD runs.
gist.impact: Internal deployment pipelines for worker apps will now succeed cleanly without failing on non-existent Pages projects.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot wearing a tiny hard hat (representing 'workers' and 'infrastructure') fixing a glowing green pipeline, with the robot companion holding a green checkmark to symbolize a successful build.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny yellow hard hat, happily tightening a glowing green pipe with a small wrench. A chunky pixel robot companion holds up a sign with a green checkmark. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel mint gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
